title: maclura greveana
intro: Maclura greveana, also known as the Madagascar osage orange, is a species of tree native to Madagascar. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Its health benefits include relief from fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Maclura greveana typically involve the use of its bark, leaves, or roots, which can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures.
